nodes.label: AI as research and brainstorming assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as writer of creative text
nodes.grounding_span: AI is a valuable tool, a great assistant, but it needs to remain an assistant

nodes.label: AI enabling multitasking and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: allows me to multitask a lot more efficiently

nodes.label: AI reliability and consistency
nodes.grounding_span: I've only ever had them have a minor hiccup here or there

nodes.label: Preserving authorial authenticity and creative enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: I do not want the AI to help with the written part in any way, shape or form

nodes.label: AI as tool for editing grammar
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't have a problem with an AI fixing my grammar because editing grammar is not fun

nodes.label: Productivity and efficient time use
nodes.grounding_span: allows me to multitask a lot more efficiently so that I can get more writing done

nodes.label: Creative fulfillment and personal accomplishment
nodes.grounding_span: the writing is the fun part. It's where the creativity really gets to shine

nodes.label: Authenticity and ethical integrity
nodes.grounding_span: it would be disingenuous to publish a book that I did not write in its entirety

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as assistant
nodes.grounding_span: As long as the AI gets the job done, I'm happy

nodes.label: Firm boundary against AI writing
nodes.grounding_span: I do not want the AI to help with the written part in any way, shape or form

nodes.label: Openness to AI for editing
nodes.grounding_span: I may attempt to use AI to help with the editing process

nodes.label: Broad-to-narrow information gathering
nodes.grounding_span: I like to start broad and that allows me to pick and choose what details I think are most important

nodes.label: Patient and consistent tool use
nodes.grounding_span: I'm a pretty patient person so perhaps that's why
